# /gmail-draft-reply

Draft Gmail reply and save as draft.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

The user names or forwards a specific thread and wants a ready-to-review reply waiting in Gmail Drafts - "reply to Sarah about the invoice and tell her Friday works", "draft a polite decline to the vendor pitch". Ideal for replies that need care (negotiations, apologies, pushback) where the user wants to edit before sending.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Which thread - sender, subject keywords, and rough date if the description is ambiguous
- Desired outcome: accept / decline / negotiate / answer questions / buy time
- Anything the reply must NOT commit to (dates, prices, scope)
- Tone: match the thread's existing register (default) or explicitly formal/casual
- Reply vs reply-all, and any recipients to add or drop

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Inbox-aware, privacy-conscious. Never send mail without explicit approval.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@gmail** - Gmail
  - MCP server key: `gmail`
  - Example tools: `gmail__search_threads`, `gmail__get_thread`, `gmail__create_draft`
  Setup: enable this app in your AI tool's connector/integration settings and sign in when prompted.

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- **Propose before write**: show a review table; wait for user to say `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.
- Never delete, send, or publish without explicit confirmation.
- Summarize every action taken at the end of the run so the user can review it.

## Workflow

1. Locate the exact thread: search by sender + subject keywords (e.g. `from:sarah subject:invoice newer_than:30d`). If more than one thread matches, list the candidates with dates and ask the user to pick - never guess.
2. Read the **full thread**, newest to oldest. Collect: every open question addressed to the user, dates or amounts under discussion, attachments referenced, and who is on the To/Cc lines.
3. Learn the user's register from their own earlier messages in the thread (greeting style, sign-off, formality, sentence length) and mirror it. If the user has never written in the thread, default to matching the counterpart's formality.
4. Draft the reply: answer **every** question that was asked; if declining or pushing back, give a brief reason and an alternative; keep it in the same language as the thread; no placeholder brackets.
5. Decide reply vs reply-all deliberately and state the final recipient list - a wrong reply-all is the costliest failure of this skill.
6. Present the draft in chat for review (see Output format) before any write.
7. On `apply`, create the draft via the Gmail connector **attached to the original thread** (pass the thread/message ID so it lands as a reply, not a detached compose). Do not send.
8. Confirm: "Draft saved to your Gmail Drafts on thread <subject> - review and send from Gmail."

## Output format

```
Thread: <subject> - last message from <sender>, <date>
Open questions they asked: 1) ... 2) ...

--- Proposed reply ---
To: <recipients>   Cc: <or none>
<full draft text, ready to send as-is>
----------------------

Commitments this draft makes: <dates, amounts, promises - or "none">
Not addressed (on purpose): <anything deferred>

Say `apply` to save as a Gmail draft (it will NOT be sent), or tell me what to change.
```

## Do

- Verify the @gmail connector is enabled and discover the exact tool names in your session before searching
- Quote the counterpart's own words when answering a specific ask ("you asked whether Friday works - yes")
- Match the user's habitual sign-off ("Best," vs "Cheers,") from their prior messages
- Call out every commitment (date, price, deliverable) the draft makes so the user consciously approves it
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if the desired outcome is genuinely unclear

## Don't

- Don't send - this skill ends at a saved draft, always
- Don't reply-all by default; justify every recipient
- Don't invent facts, prices, or availability that aren't in the thread or given by the user
- Don't create a detached draft that loses the thread - if the connector can't attach to the thread, say so and provide copy-paste text instead
- Don't leave any open question from the original email unanswered without flagging it

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@gmail) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```

# /gmail-follow-up-list

List sent Gmail with no reply needing follow-up.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

The user sends emails that quietly die - proposals, invoices, intros, favors, job applications - and wants a chase list: which sent messages never got a reply, how long they've been silent, and a ready-made nudge for each. Run daily or weekly as a "who owes me a reply" review.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Lookback window (default: sent 3-14 days ago - under 3 days is too soon to chase, over ~30 is usually stale)
- Scope: external recipients only, or include internal colleagues
- High-stakes keywords to prioritize (invoice, proposal, contract, offer)
- Threads or recipients to never chase (newsletters they replied to, no-reply addresses, sensitive threads)

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Inbox-aware, privacy-conscious. Never send mail without explicit approval.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@gmail** - Gmail
  - MCP server key: `gmail`
  - Example tools: `gmail__search_threads`, `gmail__get_thread`, `gmail__create_draft`
  Setup: enable this app in your AI tool's connector/integration settings and sign in when prompted.

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- **Propose before write**: nudge drafts are shown in a review table; wait for `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3` before creating anything.
- Nudges are saved as Gmail **drafts** on the original thread - never sent by this skill.
- Summarize every action taken at the end of the run.

## Workflow

1. Discover the Gmail tools in this session, then search sent mail in the window: `in:sent older_than:3d newer_than:14d` (adjust to the agreed range). Collect at thread level.
2. For each thread, fetch the message list and check **who wrote last**. Only threads where the user's message is the most recent count as awaiting reply. A reply that arrived after the user's message - even a one-liner - resolves the thread.
3. Filter out false positives:
   - The user's last message needed no answer ("Thanks!", "Sounds good", FYI forwards).
   - The only "reply" is an auto-responder or out-of-office → still awaiting a real reply; note the return date if the OOO states one.
   - Recipient is a no-reply/notification address.
4. Rank the queue: money and deadlines first (invoices, proposals, contracts), then external clients, then by days silent.
5. For each item, draft a 2-3 line nudge that (a) references the original ask and the date it was sent, (b) restates the specific question or deadline, (c) offers an easy out ("if X is a better contact, happy to redirect"). No guilt-tripping.
6. Present the review table; on `apply`, create the nudges as drafts on their original threads. Never send.

## Output format

```
Follow-up queue - <date>, sent mail from <range>

| # | Recipient | Subject | What you asked | Sent | Days silent | Stakes |
(sorted by stakes, then staleness)

Suggested nudges:
1. <recipient>: "<2-3 line nudge text>"
...

Skipped (and why): "thanks-only" closers (n), auto-replies pending return (n), no-reply senders (n)

Say `apply 1,3` to save those nudges as drafts on the original threads (not sent).
```

## Do

- Verify the @gmail connector and discover exact tool names before searching
- Judge "no reply" at thread level by checking the actual last sender, not by absence from the inbox
- Note out-of-office return dates and suggest deferring those nudges
- Keep nudges short and reference the concrete original ask - vague "just bumping this" gets ignored
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if the window or scope is unclear

## Don't

- Don't auto-send nudges - drafts only, gated behind `apply`
- Don't chase threads the user closed themselves ("no reply needed" messages)
- Don't chase no-reply addresses or mailing lists
- Don't assume silence in Gmail means no answer - the reply may have come by phone or chat; present the list as "no reply *in Gmail*"
- Don't stack multiple nudges on a thread that already has an unanswered nudge - flag it as "chase differently" instead

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@gmail) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)

## Schedule

Recommended cadence: **daily**

Example: `/loop 7d Run /gmail-follow-up-list`
```

Commitments are buried in email but missing from Calendar: flight confirmations, dentist appointments, delivery windows, RSVP deadlines, bill due dates. - Lookback window for mail (default: last 7 days)

```

5. Shape by type: appointments/travel → timed events with location; deadlines/RSVPs → all-day event on the due date (or timed reminder per user preference); bills → all-day event titled "Pay <biller> - <amount>".

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Who is VIP: a Google Contacts group/label (e.g. "VIP" or starred contacts), or an explicit list of names/addresses

3. Also check the reverse direction: threads where the **user's last message to a VIP is unanswered** or a **VIP's question to the user is still unreplied** - a VIP kept waiting is the most urgent line in the digest.
4. For each thread, read the latest message and record: what the VIP said or asked (one or two lines), thread state (new ask / reply to you / ongoing conversation / cc-only), any explicit date or deadline, attachments.
5. Grade urgency from concrete signals only: a question mark aimed at the user, a stated deadline, "urgent"/"EOD", or waiting >24h on the user. No vibes-based urgency.
6. Order the digest: (a) VIPs waiting on the user, (b) new asks, (c) everything else grouped per VIP. Optionally list quiet VIPs ("nothing from Priya in 9 days") if the user wants relationship-tending nudges.
